name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-30
title: test-tdd-step.py terminal-state test t8 is misleading
description: test-tdd-step.py t8 asserts that 'deprecated -> spec' is denied even with --force. The ok message reads 'deprecated is terminal (no exit even with --force)'. But the test only checks rc != 0, not WHICH error was emitted. The spec invariant should be that deprecated can transition only via --force (since spec.md says forward-only WITHOUT --force; --force overrides). tdd-step.py line 340-342 hard-codes that deprecated is a terminal exception that --force cannot override. This is a stronger constraint than the spec states. Either: tighten spec.md to explicitly state deprecated is non-exitable even with --force, or relax tdd-step.py to allow --force out of deprecated. Currently behaviour and spec disagree by omission.
